verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Main blocker: Surfline consistency is lully.

conversationalSummary.text: Tomorrow is probably one to skip. Cerro at Lunch looks like the least-bad option, but not really something worth chasing. The lack of push looks like the main issue and could easily make it messy. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam first, but unless it looks surprisingly clean, I'd save the drive.

best.windFit: WSW 15 km/h · cross-off · good wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal SSW, usable S/SW/SSE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Cerro looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 11 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and good wind at 15 km/h.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
best.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
Cerro may feel too soft without more push
